item.title: Best Pixel phone deals in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best Pixel phone deals in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The Google Pixel phones were an immediate hit upon the first release in 2016, and while the lineup has faced stiff competition from brands like Samsung in recent years, the Pixel devices are still some of the best Android phones you can buy. They're renowned for their slick operating system and great camera modules, and as such, have gained a loyal following among tech enthusiasts, photography hobbyists, and Android users. And although these smartphones aren't as pricey as many Android flagships on the market today, they can be surprisingly affordable – especially if you can smoke out some juicy Pixel phone deals.

Whether you're a photography enthusiast looking for a good phone camera to take on your adventures, an Android fan seeking seamless integration into the Google software ecosystem, or simply a regular smartphone user in search of great performance that doesn't cost a fortune, these Pixel phone deals have you covered.

Best Pixel phone deals

Google threw back the curtains on its first Pixel smartphone in 2016, and the lineup is now in its seventh generation. For this roundup, we've smoked out Pixel phone deals on current and previous-gen models, including the Pixel 6 series, Pixel 7 series, and the new Google Pixel Fold. This covers all the Pixel deals on devices that feature suitably modern hardware and are still available new, excluding any pre-owned or refurbished phones or ones that might feel a bit long in the tooth in 2023.

Google Pixel 6
$350 $599 Save $249

It's not the latest and greatest, but if you're looking for a good brand-name Android phone you can buy outright, the Google Pixel 6 is still a capable device. It features a 6.4-inch OLD display, Google's Tensor chip, and a nice camera module, making it a solid choice if you don't mind buying a last-gen flagship.

$350 at Amazon
Google Pixel 6 Pro
$790 $899 Save $109

The Pixel 6 pro is the larger sibling of the Pixel 6, featuring a higher-resolution 6.7-inch display, more RAM, a bigger battery, and a better selfie camera. You get all that on top of the top-notch Android experience that the Pixels are known for.

$790 at Amazon
Google Pixel 6a
$199 $349 Save $150

The Google Pixel 6a stands as a great mid-range alternative between a cheap Android phone and a premium flagship. For its price, it packs a number of nice features including an AMOLED display and a great camera module, even if it's not as high-end as its flagship siblings.

$344 at Amazon $199 at Google Fi Wireless
Google Pixel 7

Released in October 2022, the Pixel 7 represents a refinement of everything we love about Google's smartphone lineup. Its 6.32-inch FHD+ OLED display looks great, the Tensor G2 chip delivers great performance, and, as you'd expect, it packs a great camera setup.

$449 at Amazon Up to $740 off with trade-in Up to $500 off with trade-in Up to $599 off with trade-in $599 at Mint Mobile
Google Pixel 7 Pro
$360 $940 Save $580

The Pixel 7 Pro might be our favorite flagship phone that Google has ever released. It's big, beautiful, and powerful, boasting a gorgeous 6.7-inch QHD+ OLED display with a buttery smooth 120Hz refresh rate. The hardware under the hood is just as impressive, as is its excellent camera module.

$854 at Amazon Up to $600 off with trade-in $360 at AT&T
Google Pixel 7a
$180 $520 Save $340

Google has come close to perfecting its budget-friendly A-series phones with the Pixel 7a, which stands as the best mid-range Android flagship alternative on the market right now. It packs the same Tensor G2 chip as its pricier siblings, but doesn't cut too many corners with a 6.1-inch OLED display and a very good camera setup.

$180 at AT&T Up to $500 off with trade-in $499 at Google Fi Wireless
Google Pixel Fold
$1000 $1800 Save $800

Foldable phones are back in a big way, and Google is getting in on this trend with the new Pixel Fold. It's a great first attempt and a worthy buy for Pixel fans. It's a fantastic size, whether folded or unfolded, and in true Pixel fashion, it's got the best camera of all the folding phones we've used.

Up to $1,000 off with trade-in Up to $1,000 off with trade-in $1000 at T-Mobile Up to $1,000 off with trade-in

FAQ

Q: When can you get the best savings on Pixel phones?

The most promising opportunities to score Pixel phone deals typically come during major shopping events and seasonal sales like Prime Day and Black Friday. Also, keep an eye out for other holiday sales and back-to-school promotions. Additionally, Google generally introduces new Pixel models every year, which leads to price drops on previous-gen models. These releases are perfect opportunities to score a deal on a great phone if you don't need the latest release.

Q: Where can you get the best savings?

Pixel phone deals are available across big online retailers like Amazon and Best Buy. Official Google Store promotions can also offer direct discounts, trade-in deals, and occasional freebies. Major wireless carriers frequently run enticing Pixel phone deals, often including trade-in options and exclusive bundles, to lure in new customers. Don't hesitate to take advantage of these if you're shopping for a new phone plan or looking to switch service providers, as this is often where you'll score the biggest savings (or even get a new phone for free).

Q: How can you tell if it's a good deal?

Determining whether a particular Pixel phone deal is worthwhile calls for a bit of research and comparison shopping. First, check the retail cost and the price history of the specific Pixel model you're interested in to gauge how much you're saving compared to its regular sticker price. Suppose your Pixel phone deal is tied to a trade-in offer and/or a new cellular plan. In that case, it's also essential to read the fine print and understand the terms or conditions associated with the deal, such as trade-in requirements or activation commitments. Online reviews and customer feedback also provide insights into the quality of the deal. Finally, you can check out price trackers like CamelCamelCamel and Keepa to see pricing history on Amazon.
item.description:
The Google Pixel phones were an immediate hit upon the first release in 2016, and while the lineup has faced stiff competition from brands like Samsung in recent years, the Pixel devices are still some of the best Android phones you can buy. They're renowned for their slick operating system and great camera modules, and as such, have gained a loyal following among tech enthusiasts, photography hobbyists, and Android users. And although these smartphones aren't as pricey as many Android flagships on the market today, they can be surprisingly affordable – especially if you can smoke out some juicy Pixel phone deals.

Whether you're a photography enthusiast looking for a good phone camera to take on your adventures, an Android fan seeking seamless integration into the Google software ecosystem, or simply a regular smartphone user in search of great performance that doesn't cost a fortune, these Pixel phone deals have you covered.

Best Pixel phone deals

Google threw back the curtains on its first Pixel smartphone in 2016, and the lineup is now in its seventh generation. For this roundup, we've smoked out Pixel phone deals on current and previous-gen models, including the Pixel 6 series, Pixel 7 series, and the new Google Pixel Fold. This covers all the Pixel deals on devices that feature suitably modern hardware and are still available new, excluding any pre-owned or refurbished phones or ones that might feel a bit long in the tooth in 2023.

Google Pixel 6
$350 $599 Save $249

It's not the latest and greatest, but if you're looking for a good brand-name Android phone you can buy outright, the Google Pixel 6 is still a capable device. It features a 6.4-inch OLD display, Google's Tensor chip, and a nice camera module, making it a solid choice if you don't mind buying a last-gen flagship.

$350 at Amazon

item.title: Don't miss out on this Razer 27-inch 165Hz gaming monitor down to its lowest price ever
item.itunes:title: Don't miss out on this Razer 27-inch 165Hz gaming monitor down to its lowest price ever
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Razer Raptor 27-inch Monitor
$300 $800 Save $500

The Razer Raptor 27-inch gaming monitor has high-end specs for gamers, including adaptive refresh rates and Quad HD resolution, plus it has a premium design with RGB and cable management.

$300 at Newegg

Gaming monitors can be expensive, but if you know where to look, they don't have to be. Today, we're spotlighting a fantastic deal on Razer's Raptor gaming monitor. This monitor offers fantastic features like 1440p resolution, 165Hz refresh rate, 1ms response time, HDR 400, and more. In addition, you also get wonderful construction with the monitor's aluminum base and built-in cable management.

Now, for a limited time, you can score a great deal on the Razer 27-inch Raptor gaming monitor that comes priced at just $299.99, which is $500 off its normal price, bringing it down to a historic low. So if you've been thinking about grabbing a new monitor and wanted one that offers excellent construction and specifications, this is the model for you.

What's great about the Razer Raptor 27-inch gaming monitor?

This monitor is a fantastic option thanks to its excellent construction and wonderful specifications. The monitor relies on a beautiful 27-inch IPS panel that provides accurate colors and contrast covering 95% DCI-P3 color gamut and delivering HDR400 certification. In addition, the 1440p panel offers a quick refresh rate and response time, coming in at 165Hz and 1ms respectively.

Furthermore, the monitor also offers support for Nvidia's G-Sync and AMD's FreeSync Premium for smooth graphical processing even in the toughest conditions. While it's hard to tell from the images, the monitor also provides built-in cable management in the rear, with channels that can handle cable routing for all your connected devices.

For the most part, you really can't go wrong with this monitor. It offers excellent visuals, solid construction, and comes priced well below retail in this limited-time sale ahead of Black Friday.

item.title: Best ASRock motherboards in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best ASRock motherboards in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
ASRock is responsible for creating some of the best motherboards on the market, though simply matching the CPU socket isn't all one needs to consider when buying a main board. Other factors to look at include voltage regulator modules (and their cooling), the number of implemented power phases for CPU power delivery, and connectivity to name but a few. If you're already set on buying an ASRock motherboard, we've rounded up our favorites to help you get started.

  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock Z790 Taichi
    Best Intel
    $490 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock X670E Taichi
    Best AMD
    $400 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock Z790 LiveMixer
    Best for content creators
    $200 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock WRX80 Creator
    Best for enthusiasts
    $900 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi
    Best value Intel
    $278 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi
    Best budget AMD
    $235 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock Z790 PG-ITX/TB4
    Best compact Intel
    $285 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock B650E PG-ITX
    Best Compact AMD
    $290 at Newegg

The very best ASRock motherboards in 2023

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 Taichi
Best Intel

Our favorite ASRock Intel motherboard

When you work and play on the same workstation, the ASRock Z790 Taichi is a solid foundation for both. It has almost everything you could ask for in an Intel PC motherboard.

Brand
ASrock
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-7000
Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
Z790
Socket
LGA 1700
Power Phases
24+1+2
PCI Slots
3
Storage
4x M.2, 8x SATA
Audio
Realtek ALC4082
Networking
2.5GbE, Wi-Fi 6E
Internal I/O
2x USB 2.0, 2x USB 3.2 Gen1, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2x2
Rear I/O
2x Thunderbolt 4, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, 6x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-A 2.0, 1x HDMI
$579 at Amazon $490 at Newegg

If you're looking for the best Z790 motherboard for a high-end ASRock PC build, then we think the ASRock Z790 Taichi is as good as it gets. It's packed with excellent features and delivers reliable performance, building a solid platform for a high-end build. This particular motherboard features a 24+1+4 phase SPS Dr.MOS power design. It's capable of handling even the most demanding CPUs in the Raptor Lake series like the Intel Core i9-13900K. The Z790 Taichi also supports overclocking and the VRM heatsink ensures everything stays cool at all times. In addition to the RGB lights, the VRM heatsink cover and the chipset heatsink cover, both feature a unique bronze-colored gear element, exclusive to the Taichi motherboard series. Supporting the best Intel processors, the Taichi comes with four DIMM slots that let you install up to 128GB of DDR5 memory modules with speeds of up to 7200MT/s supported.

You get three full-sized reinforced x16 slots on the board in addition to an x1 slot. There are only three M.2 slots for faster SSDs, but you can install additional SATA drives with the available 6Gb/s connections. The M.2 slots are hidden under the heatsink, which should help keep the installed drives adequately cooled. The ASRock Z790 Taichi comes with a pre-installed IO shield at the back and it covers a ton of different ports. You get as many as eight USB ports at the back including two Thunderbolt 4 USB Type-C ports. There's also an HDMI port for those who want to boot without a discrete GPU. You also get two ethernet ports along with a full stack of audio ports. As a premium board, you also get plenty of headers for additional USB ports, RGB lights, fans, pumps, and more.

Source: ASRock
ASRock X670E Taichi
Best AMD

Going with AMD processors? This is the ASRock board for you.

The ASRock X670E Taichi is a high-end EATX motherboard with excellent build quality and a massive 24-stage VRM. It has support for high-speed DDR5 memory, PCIe 5.0 graphics, and multiple NVME SSDs.

Brand
ASRock
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-6600
Form Factor
E-ATX
Chipset
AMD X670E
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
24+2+1
PCI Slots
2
Storage
1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 3x PCIe 4.0 M.2, 8x SATA
Audio
Realtek ALC4082
Networking
2.5 GbE, Wi-Fi 6
Internal I/O
2x USB-A 2.0, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen1, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2x2
Rear I/O
2x USB-C 4.0, 5x USB-A 3.2 Gen2, 3x USB-A 3.2 Gen1
$400 at Newegg $415 at Micro Center

Moving onto AMD, we're recommending almost the same motherboard. The ASRock X670E Taichi could largely be considered the AMD equivalent to the Intel chipset-touting board we recommended above for Intel processors. It shares many of the same features and ports, and has the same gorgeous industrial design. It's among the best ASRock motherboards you can buy right now for AMD Ryzen processors that aren't part of the Threadripper family. Just like the Intel ASRock Taichi, the company packed this motherboard full of features. Let's start with the power delivery design, which includes a 24+2+1 phase design setup. This is more than enough to handle even the most power-hungry AMD CPUs such as the mighty AMD Ryzen 9 7950X. Then there are the usual four DIMM slots for system memory. These are capable of handling up to 128GB of DDR5-6600+.

Two PCIe 5.0 x16 slots are at hand for the most powerful graphics cards and an additional x16 slot is good to go for any expansion cards you may require. Audio is handled by the Realtek ALC4082 with an additional ESS SABRE9218 DAC for good measure. If all that wasn't enough, there are a total of four M.2 SSD slots, one being PCIe 5.0 and the rest PCIe 4.0. Eight SATA 3.0 ports are available for hooking up all 2.5-inch and 3.5-inch storage drives. Networking is lightning-fast too with Killer branding throughout the board. We've got 2.5Gb Ethernet and 802.11ax Wi-Fi 6E with Bluetooth 5.3. Connectivity is sorted too, thanks to the two USB-C 4.0, five USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, and three USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports. Just like the Intel Z790 Taichi motherboard, this is about as good as you can get with AMD and ASRock. It has the power, connectivity, and BIOS support for you to create a powerful PC.

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 LiveMixer
Best for content creators

This board will help you become the next Twitch hit

The ASRock Z790 LiveMixer, as the name suggests, is designed with creators in mind. The design is whacky, trendy, and fit for the 2020 decade. If you're building a PC to create content on, this may be just what you're looking for in terms of motherboards.

Brand
ASRock
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-7200
Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
Intel Z790
Socket
LGA 1700
Power Phases
14+1+1
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0, 2x PCIe 4.0
Storage
1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 4x PCIe 4.0 M.2, 4x SATA
Audio
Realtek ALC897
Networking
2.5 GbE
Internal I/O
1x Thunderbolt, 2x USB 3.2 Gen1, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2x2,2x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
2x USB 3.2 Gen2 Type-A Ports (10 Gb/s) - 2 x USB 3.2 Gen2 Type-C Ports (10 Gb/s) - 4 x USB 3.2 Gen1 Ports (USB32_23 are Lightning Gaming Ports. USB32_45 support Ultra USB Power.) - 6 x USB 2.0 Ports
$200 at Newegg $250 at Amazon

Don't write off this motherboard for being a mistake. The design may be polarizing, but that's how manufacturers are attempting to differentiate their products. Gaming-focused motherboards have more RGB lighting than a local airstrip and it seems graffiti artists are now being thrown into the mix with digital content creators. The end result is the whacky ASrock Z790 LiveMixer. This board is designed for content creators, video & photo editing, and game development. Because this motherboard comes with the Intel Z790 chipset and LGA 1700 socket for 12th and 13th Gen Intel processors, ASRock went all out with the RAM and PCI slots. You'll need DDR5 memory for this motherboard, supporting up to DDR5-6800. To keep everything operating stably, ASRock deployed a 14+1+1 phase power delivery system and uses high-quality Japanese capacitors.

The impressive Realtek ALC897 codec and Nahimic Audio are present too so you know just how much kit the company has thrown on. The ASRock Z790 LiveMixer makes good use of the Dragon RTL8125BG networking stack to unlock a single 2.5Gb Ethernet port. There's no Wi-Fi out of the box, but an M.2 (Key E) Socket is available for installing such a card to add wireless capabilities. With content creation, it's not uncommon to work with huge files over the network, which is where the wired connection comes into play with less latency and better performance. As well as the Key E Socket, ASRock added four PCI slots for connecting all your GPUs, capture cards, and/or expansion cards.

There's one PCIe 5.0 x16 slot, followed by two PCIe 4.0 x16 slots, and an additional PCIe 3.0 x1 slot. A total of five M.2 SSD slots are at hand with one supporting PCIe 5.0 storage. There's potential for plenty of storage capacity thanks to the four SATA 3 ports available for older 2.5- or 3.5-inch drives. The rear I/O stack is also unique for the ASRock Z790 LiveMixer motherboard, consisting of an HDMI port, DisplayPort (1.4), optical SPDIF out, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, two USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 ports, four USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, and six USB 2.0 ports. If you've got a lot of equipment to hook up to the PC, this motherboard can handle it all and then some.

Source: ASRock
ASRock WRX80 Creator
Best for enthusiasts

Upgrade your PC (and life) and AMD Ryzen Threadripper

The ASRock WRX80 is a monster of a motherboard designed specifically for AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3000 and 5000 series processors. Looking to build a powerful workstation or server? Start your build right here.

The ASRock WRX80 is an absolute monster of a motherboard. It's designed for heavy, intense applications and enthusiasts with support for the latest AMD Ryzen Threadripper processors. This thing is unlike any other motherboard we're recommending in this guide as it is capable of serving up to 64 lanes of PCIe 4.0 and has a dual-fan heatsink covering eight 105A VRM phases. Then there's another 50mm fan for the chipset. Rocking an AMD sWRX socket, you'll be able to install AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3000 and 5000 series processors. Two M.2 PCIe 4.0 slots are available for rapid storage and a further seven PCIe 4.0 x16 slots can be used for just about anything your imagination is capable of conjuring up.

It's a serious motherboard with not a drop of RGB lighting in sight. You may have noticed the eight DIMM array of RAM slots, which is capable of handling up to 2048GB (2TB!) of DDR4-4533+, and yes that capacity is not a typo. ASRock is so serious about the WRX80 Creator R2.0, the company used a 14-layer server-grade PCB, which is unheard of in the consumer motherboard segment, but this motherboard comes at a cost. The rear I/O is also incredible. You'll have the choice of using two USB-C 4.0 ports, two Mini-DisplayPort ports, four USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, and two 10Gb LAN ports. There's going to be very little you can throw at this motherboard and cause problems. If you're looking to build a powerful HEDT PC, look no further than the ASRock WRX80 CREATOR R2.0.

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi
Best value Intel

Save money with ASRock's affordable Intel platform

The ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi is designed for those who want a decent motherboard without spending too much money. It has all the right features you'll need for a capable 12th or 13th Gen Intel PC build.

We've covered some of the more powerful and even enthusiast-grade motherboard offerings from ASRock, but the company has some more affordable options available. Take the ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi, for instance. It undercuts many of the other motherboards in this ASRock collection by a substantial amount but supports 12th and 13th Gen Intel processors. The 16+1+1 phase power delivery design may not be the most powerful around, but it's still more than enough for even the Intel Core i9-13900K.

Even though this is slightly cheaper than other Z790 boards, there's still support for DDR5 RAM with speeds up to 7200MT/s. There's 2.5Gb networking, which is a nice touch and usually reserved for more expensive motherboards, even in 2023. For PCI slots, ASRock went with a three x16-slot configuration with one supporting PCIe 5.0 speeds, the second PCIe 4.0, and the third PCIe 3.0. As a bonus, there's a single M.2 (Key E) Socket for WiFi modules (it's already populated with this model).

For storage, we're looking at eight SATA 3 ports for 2.5- and 3.5-inch drives, as well as five M.2 slots for modern super-fast PCIe SSDs. One of these M.2 slots supports PCIe 5.0 speeds while the remaining four are PCIe 4.0 "only." The rear panel is fully stacked too, rocking an HDMI port, DisplayPort, optical SPDIF, one USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 port, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, and eight USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports. You may be saving some money with the ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi, but it has all the important features your PC will need.

Source: ASRock
ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi
Best budget AMD

This AMD motherboard won't set fire to your wallet

You'll notice the ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi price before anything else and that shouldn't lead you to a false assumption that this motherboard won't have many of the more advanced features. It's far greater than its price tag would lead you to believe.

As well as buying more affordable motherboards for Intel processors, there are numerous budget-friendlier options for AMD too. We're picking the mighty ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi for our value recommendation. It has the slightly less headline-catching B650 chipset, compared to the flagship X670, but it's still worth checking out for a powerful AMD Ryzen 7000 series PC build. The design is almost identical to the Intel version we also recommended but with a few notable element alterations. It's a good-looking board.

To handle the more capable AMD Ryzen processors (as well as supporting some degree of overclocking), ASRock implemented a 16+2+1 phase power delivery system. The four DIMM slots are capable of handling up to DDR5-6600 RAM, which already allows for one powerful system. There may only be two x16 PCI slots, but one is running at PCIe 5.0 speeds for the latest graphics cards. Three M.2 slots are ready to go for super-fast SSD storage and one of these is also capable of handling PCIe 5.0.

Rear I/O is also a strong point, considering the price. You'll be able to play with a single HDMI port, one DisplayPort, one Optical SPDIF, one USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 port, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, four USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, and two USB-A 2.0 ports. And speaking of connectivity, there's Wi-Fi 6E and 2.5Gb Ethernet to get you online. The ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi shows you don't have to spend a small fortune on a motherboard to enjoy a solid experience with the latest AMD processors.

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 PG-ITX/TB4
Best compact Intel

Build your dream compact gaming PC with this Mini-ITX offering

$335 $420 Save $85

ASRock's Z790 PG-ITX/TB4 is a motherboard that punches way above its weight. You'll be limited by RAM capacity since there are only two DIMM slots, but it has an impressive list of features for compact PC builds.

When building a compact PC using a small form factor (SFF) case, your choice of motherboards will be limited. We're choosing the ASRock Z790 PG-ITX/TB4 as our recommendation for the Mini-ITX Intel motherboard form factor. It's a very powerful yet compact motherboard with a 10+1+1 phase power delivery design, which should be more than enough for even the Intel Core i9-13900K. The flagship Intel Z790 chipset and LGA 1700 socket ensure you're using the very best on offer.

Because of the limited size, Mini-ITX motherboards such as this only come with two DIMM slots for RAM. This means you'll only be able to install up to 64GB of capacity, but at least there's support for up to DDR5-7000. There's also one PCI slot that's capable of running at PCIe 5.0 speeds. Fear not if you have more than one M.2 SSD as this tiny motherboard actually has three M.2 slots, all three of them running at PCIe 4.0 speeds.

For networking, you'll be able to rely on either WiFi 6E or 2.5Gb Ethernet. Rear I/O isn't too shabby either. One HDMI port is joined by DisplayPort, Optical SPDIF, two USB-C 4.0 ports, and six USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports. You can build a very capable system using this motherboard with the same excellent BIOS you'd expect from ASRock.

Source: ASRock
ASRock B650E PG-ITX
Best Compact AMD

This AMD motherboard won't set fire to your wallet

The ASRock B650E PG-ITX  is an impressive compact motherboard with two M.2 slots, PCIe 5.0 support across the board, two DDR5 DIMM slots, and plenty of USB connectivity.

$322 at Amazon $290 at Newegg

The ASRock B650E PG-ITX is our pick for the best compact ASRock AMD motherboard. It has the B650 chipset and AM5 socket, meaning you'll need to use the latest and greatest AMD processors with this motherboard, as well as DDR5 RAM. Some may view the lack of DDR4 RAM support as a drawback for AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors, but it's faster and vastly more affordable than when the generation launched. Throw in a single PCIe 5.0 x16 slot for a graphics card and two M.2 drive bays and you've got one capable compact motherboard. Being a Mini-ITX board, you will be limited by the two DIMM slots, supporting a maximum of 64GB of DDR5-6400 RAM.

Other highlights of the ASRock B650E PG-ITX include a 10+2+1 phase power delivery design, which is pretty solid for such a small motherboard. It won't allow for some record-breaking overclocking results with an AMD Ryzen 9 7950X, but it will be enough to provide reliable power at near-stock settings. This allows for a powerful, yet compact AMD system to be built. Install an AMD or Nvidia GPU from recent generations and you'll easily handle many of the latest and most-demanding PC games. 2.5GbE and Wi-Fi 6E will have you online in moments after installing your favorite OS and there are ample USB ports on the rear of the ASRock B650E PG-ITX for hooking up all your peripherals.

Choosing the best ASRock motherboard

ASRock has a lot of other motherboards in addition to the ones we've listed above. However, these are the ones we think are worth considering. We've added many Z790 motherboards like the ASRock Z790 Taichi for those looking to build a new PC based on Intel's Alder Lake and Raptor Lake CPUs. The ASRock X670E Taichi is also a great motherboard for builds with AMD processors. If you want the absolute best performance available, you're going to need an AMD Ryzen Threadripper with the mighty ASRock WRX80 Creator R2.0.

You can also check out our collection of the best LGA 1700 motherboards if you want to build a new PC with one of the new 12th or 13th-generation Intel Core processors. In that case, don't forget to take a look at our LGA 1700 CPU coolers list to find a good cooler for the new chips. Be sure to let us know what you think about our recommendations by dropping a line below. You can also join our XDA Computing Forums to get more product recommendations from our expert community.

Source: ASRock

item.description:
ASRock is responsible for creating some of the best motherboards on the market, though simply matching the CPU socket isn't all one needs to consider when buying a main board. Other factors to look at include voltage regulator modules (and their cooling), the number of implemented power phases for CPU power delivery, and connectivity to name but a few. If you're already set on buying an ASRock motherboard, we've rounded up our favorites to help you get started.

The very best ASRock motherboards in 2023

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 Taichi
Best Intel

Our favorite ASRock Intel motherboard

When you work and play on the same workstation, the ASRock Z790 Taichi is a solid foundation for both. It has almost everything you could ask for in an Intel PC motherboard.

$579 at Amazon $490 at Newegg

If you're looking for the best Z790 motherboard for a high-end ASRock PC build, then we think the ASRock Z790 Taichi is as good as it gets. It's packed with excellent features and delivers reliable performance, building a solid platform for a high-end build. This particular motherboard features a 24+1+4 phase SPS Dr.MOS power design. It's capable of handling even the most demanding CPUs in the Raptor Lake series like the Intel Core i9-13900K. The Z790 Taichi also supports overclocking and the VRM heatsink ensures everything stays cool at all times. In addition to the RGB lights, the VRM heatsink cover and the chipset heatsink cover, both feature a unique bronze-colored gear element, exclusive to the Taichi motherboard series. Supporting the best Intel processors, the Taichi comes with four DIMM slots that let you install up to 128GB of DDR5 memory modules with speeds of up to 7200MT/s supported.

You get three full-sized reinforced x16 slots on the board in addition to an x1 slot. There are only three M.2 slots for faster SSDs, but you can install additional SATA drives with the available 6Gb/s connections. The M.2 slots are hidden under the heatsink, which should help keep the installed drives adequately cooled. The ASRock Z790 Taichi comes with a pre-installed IO shield at the back and it covers a ton of different ports. You get as many as eight USB ports at the back including two Thunderbolt 4 USB Type-C ports. There's also an HDMI port for those who want to boot without a discrete GPU. You also get two ethernet ports along with a full stack of audio ports. As a premium board, you also get plenty of headers for additional USB ports, RGB lights, fans, pumps, and more.

Source: ASRock
ASRock X670E Taichi
Best AMD

Going with AMD processors? This is the ASRock board for you.

The ASRock X670E Taichi is a high-end EATX motherboard with excellent build quality and a massive 24-stage VRM. It has support for high-speed DDR5 memory, PCIe 5.0 graphics, and multiple NVME SSDs.

$400 at Newegg $415 at Micro Center

Moving onto AMD, we're recommending almost the same motherboard. The ASRock X670E Taichi could largely be considered the AMD equivalent to the Intel chipset-touting board we recommended above for Intel processors. It shares many of the same features and ports, and has the same gorgeous industrial design. It's among the best ASRock motherboards you can buy right now for AMD Ryzen processors that aren't part of the Threadripper family. Just like the Intel ASRock Taichi, the company packed this motherboard full of features. Let's start with the power delivery design, which includes a 24+2+1 phase design setup. This is more than enough to handle even the most power-hungry AMD CPUs such as the mighty AMD Ryzen 9 7950X. Then there are the usual four DIMM slots for system memory. These are capable of handling up to 128GB of DDR5-6600+.

Two PCIe 5.0 x16 slots are at hand for the most powerful graphics cards and an additional x16 slot is good to go for any expansion cards you may require. Audio is handled by the Realtek ALC4082 with an additional ESS SABRE9218 DAC for good measure. If all that wasn't enough, there are a total of four M.2 SSD slots, one being PCIe 5.0 and the rest PCIe 4.0. Eight SATA 3.0 ports are available for hooking up all 2.5-inch and 3.5-inch storage drives. Networking is lightning-fast too with Killer branding throughout the board. We've got 2.5Gb Ethernet and 802.11ax Wi-Fi 6E with Bluetooth 5.3. Connectivity is sorted too, thanks to the two USB-C 4.0, five USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, and three USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports. Just like the Intel Z790 Taichi motherboard, this is about as good as you can get with AMD and ASRock. It has the power, connectivity, and BIOS support for you to create a powerful PC.

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 LiveMixer
Best for content creators

This board will help you become the next Twitch hit

The ASRock Z790 LiveMixer, as the name suggests, is designed with creators in mind. The design is whacky, trendy, and fit for the 2020 decade. If you're building a PC to create content on, this may be just what you're looking for in terms of motherboards.

$200 at Newegg $250 at Amazon

Don't write off this motherboard for being a mistake. The design may be polarizing, but that's how manufacturers are attempting to differentiate their products. Gaming-focused motherboards have more RGB lighting than a local airstrip and it seems graffiti artists are now being thrown into the mix with digital content creators. The end result is the whacky ASrock Z790 LiveMixer. This board is designed for content creators, video & photo editing, and game development. Because this motherboard comes with the Intel Z790 chipset and LGA 1700 socket for 12th and 13th Gen Intel processors, ASRock went all out with the RAM and PCI slots. You'll need DDR5 memory for this motherboard, supporting up to DDR5-6800. To keep everything operating stably, ASRock deployed a 14+1+1 phase power delivery system and uses high-quality Japanese capacitors.

The impressive Realtek ALC897 codec and Nahimic Audio are present too so you know just how much kit the company has thrown on. The ASRock Z790 LiveMixer makes good use of the Dragon RTL8125BG networking stack to unlock a single 2.5Gb Ethernet port. There's no Wi-Fi out of the box, but an M.2 (Key E) Socket is available for installing such a card to add wireless capabilities. With content creation, it's not uncommon to work with huge files over the network, which is where the wired connection comes into play with less latency and better performance. As well as the Key E Socket, ASRock added four PCI slots for connecting all your GPUs, capture cards, and/or expansion cards.

There's one PCIe 5.0 x16 slot, followed by two PCIe 4.0 x16 slots, and an additional PCIe 3.0 x1 slot. A total of five M.2 SSD slots are at hand with one supporting PCIe 5.0 storage. There's potential for plenty of storage capacity thanks to the four SATA 3 ports available for older 2.5- or 3.5-inch drives. The rear I/O stack is also unique for the ASRock Z790 LiveMixer motherboard, consisting of an HDMI port, DisplayPort (1.4), optical SPDIF out, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, two USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 ports, four USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, and six USB 2.0 ports. If you've got a lot of equipment to hook up to the PC, this motherboard can handle it all and then some.

Source: ASRock

Upgrade your PC (and life) and AMD Ryzen Threadripper

The ASRock WRX80 is a monster of a motherboard designed specifically for AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3000 and 5000 series processors. Looking to build a powerful workstation or server? Start your build right here.

1x Thunderbolt, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen1, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2x2, 2x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
1x USB-A 3.2 Gen2, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2, 8x USB-A 3.2 Gen1
$315 at Amazon $278 at Newegg

We've covered some of the more powerful and even enthusiast-grade motherboard offerings from ASRock, but the company has some more affordable options available. Take the ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi, for instance. It undercuts many of the other motherboards in this ASRock collection by a substantial amount but supports 12th and 13th Gen Intel processors. The 16+1+1 phase power delivery design may not be the most powerful around, but it's still more than enough for even the Intel Core i9-13900K.

Even though this is slightly cheaper than other Z790 boards, there's still support for DDR5 RAM with speeds up to 7200MT/s. There's 2.5Gb networking, which is a nice touch and usually reserved for more expensive motherboards, even in 2023. For PCI slots, ASRock went with a three x16-slot configuration with one supporting PCIe 5.0 speeds, the second PCIe 4.0, and the third PCIe 3.0. As a bonus, there's a single M.2 (Key E) Socket for WiFi modules (it's already populated with this model).

For storage, we're looking at eight SATA 3 ports for 2.5- and 3.5-inch drives, as well as five M.2 slots for modern super-fast PCIe SSDs. One of these M.2 slots supports PCIe 5.0 speeds while the remaining four are PCIe 4.0 "only." The rear panel is fully stacked too, rocking an HDMI port, DisplayPort, optical SPDIF, one USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 port, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, and eight USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports. You may be saving some money with the ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi, but it has all the important features your PC will need.

Source: ASRock
ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi
Best budget AMD

This AMD motherboard won't set fire to your wallet

You'll notice the ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi price before anything else and that shouldn't lead you to a false assumption that this motherboard won't have many of the more advanced features. It's far greater than its price tag would lead you to believe.

Brand
ASRock
Memory Slots
4

AM5

Rear I/O
1x USB-A 3.2 Gen2, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2, 4x USB-A 3.2 Gen1, 2x USB-A 2.0
$235 at Newegg $235 at Amazon

As well as buying more affordable motherboards for Intel processors, there are numerous budget-friendlier options for AMD too. We're picking the mighty ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi for our value recommendation. It has the slightly less headline-catching B650 chipset, compared to the flagship X670, but it's still worth checking out for a powerful AMD Ryzen 7000 series PC build. The design is almost identical to the Intel version we also recommended but with a few notable element alterations. It's a good-looking board.

To handle the more capable AMD Ryzen processors (as well as supporting some degree of overclocking), ASRock implemented a 16+2+1 phase power delivery system. The four DIMM slots are capable of handling up to DDR5-6600 RAM, which already allows for one powerful system. There may only be two x16 PCI slots, but one is running at PCIe 5.0 speeds for the latest graphics cards. Three M.2 slots are ready to go for super-fast SSD storage and one of these is also capable of handling PCIe 5.0.

Rear I/O is also a strong point, considering the price. You'll be able to play with a single HDMI port, one DisplayPort, one Optical SPDIF, one USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 port, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, four USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, and two USB-A 2.0 ports. And speaking of connectivity, there's Wi-Fi 6E and 2.5Gb Ethernet to get you online. The ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi shows you don't have to spend a small fortune on a motherboard to enjoy a solid experience with the latest AMD processors.

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 PG-ITX/TB4
Best compact Intel

Build your dream compact gaming PC with this Mini-ITX offering

$335 $420 Save $85

ASRock's Z790 PG-ITX/TB4 is a motherboard that punches way above its weight. You'll be limited by RAM capacity since there are only two DIMM slots, but it has an impressive list of features for compact PC builds.

Brand
ASRock

DDR5-7000

Rear I/O

$335 at Amazon $285 at Newegg

When building a compact PC using a small form factor (SFF) case, your choice of motherboards will be limited. We're choosing the ASRock Z790 PG-ITX/TB4 as our recommendation for the Mini-ITX Intel motherboard form factor. It's a very powerful yet compact motherboard with a 10+1+1 phase power delivery design, which should be more than enough for even the Intel Core i9-13900K. The flagship Intel Z790 chipset and LGA 1700 socket ensure you're using the very best on offer.

Because of the limited size, Mini-ITX motherboards such as this only come with two DIMM slots for RAM. This means you'll only be able to install up to 64GB of capacity, but at least there's support for up to DDR5-7000. There's also one PCI slot that's capable of running at PCIe 5.0 speeds. Fear not if you have more than one M.2 SSD as this tiny motherboard actually has three M.2 slots, all three of them running at PCIe 4.0 speeds.

For networking, you'll be able to rely on either WiFi 6E or 2.5Gb Ethernet. Rear I/O isn't too shabby either. One HDMI port is joined by DisplayPort, Optical SPDIF, two USB-C 4.0 ports, and six USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports. You can build a very capable system using this motherboard with the same excellent BIOS you'd expect from ASRock.

Source: ASRock
ASRock B650E PG-ITX
Best Compact AMD

This AMD motherboard won't set fire to your wallet

The ASRock B650E PG-ITX  is an impressive compact motherboard with two M.2 slots, PCIe 5.0 support across the board, two DDR5 DIMM slots, and plenty of USB connectivity.

Brand
ASRock
Memory Slots
2

Internal I/O

$322 at Amazon $290 at Newegg

The ASRock B650E PG-ITX is our pick for the best compact ASRock AMD motherboard. It has the B650 chipset and AM5 socket, meaning you'll need to use the latest and greatest AMD processors with this motherboard, as well as DDR5 RAM. Some may view the lack of DDR4 RAM support as a drawback for AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors, but it's faster and vastly more affordable than when the generation launched. Throw in a single PCIe 5.0 x16 slot for a graphics card and two M.2 drive bays and you've got one capable compact motherboard. Being a Mini-ITX board, you will be limited by the two DIMM slots, supporting a maximum of 64GB of DDR5-6400 RAM.

Other highlights of the ASRock B650E PG-ITX include a 10+2+1 phase power delivery design, which is pretty solid for such a small motherboard. It won't allow for some record-breaking overclocking results with an AMD Ryzen 9 7950X, but it will be enough to provide reliable power at near-stock settings. This allows for a powerful, yet compact AMD system to be built. Install an AMD or Nvidia GPU from recent generations and you'll easily handle many of the latest and most-demanding PC games. 2.5GbE and Wi-Fi 6E will have you online in moments after installing your favorite OS and there are ample USB ports on the rear of the ASRock B650E PG-ITX for hooking up all your peripherals.

Choosing the best ASRock motherboard

ASRock has a lot of other motherboards in addition to the ones we've listed above. However, these are the ones we think are worth considering. We've added many Z790 motherboards like the ASRock Z790 Taichi for those looking to build a new PC based on Intel's Alder Lake and Raptor Lake CPUs. The ASRock X670E Taichi is also a great motherboard for builds with AMD processors. If you want the absolute best performance available, you're going to need an AMD Ryzen Threadripper with the mighty ASRock WRX80 Creator R2.0.

You can also check out our collection of the best LGA 1700 motherboards if you want to build a new PC with one of the new 12th or 13th-generation Intel Core processors. In that case, don't forget to take a look at our LGA 1700 CPU coolers list to find a good cooler for the new chips. Be sure to let us know what you think about our recommendations by dropping a line below. You can also join our XDA Computing Forums to get more product recommendations from our expert community.

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 Taichi
Best Intel

When you work and play on the same workstation, the ASRock Z790 Taichi is a solid foundation for both. It has almost everything you could ask for in an Intel PC motherboard.

$579 at Amazon $490 at Newegg

Going with AMD processors? This is the ASRock board for you.

The ASRock X670E Taichi is a high-end EATX motherboard with excellent build quality and a massive 24-stage VRM. It has support for high-speed DDR5 memory, PCIe 5.0 graphics, and multiple NVME SSDs.

item.content:encoded:
ASRock is responsible for creating some of the best motherboards on the market, though simply matching the CPU socket isn't all one needs to consider when buying a main board. Other factors to look at include voltage regulator modules (and their cooling), the number of implemented power phases for CPU power delivery, and connectivity to name but a few. If you're already set on buying an ASRock motherboard, we've rounded up our favorites to help you get started.

  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock Z790 Taichi
    Best Intel
    $490 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock X670E Taichi
    Best AMD
    $400 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock Z790 LiveMixer
    Best for content creators
    $200 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock WRX80 Creator
    Best for enthusiasts
    $900 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi
    Best value Intel
    $278 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi
    Best budget AMD
    $235 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock Z790 PG-ITX/TB4
    Best compact Intel
    $285 at Newegg
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock B650E PG-ITX
    Best Compact AMD
    $290 at Newegg

The very best ASRock motherboards in 2023

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 Taichi
Best Intel

Our favorite ASRock Intel motherboard

When you work and play on the same workstation, the ASRock Z790 Taichi is a solid foundation for both. It has almost everything you could ask for in an Intel PC motherboard.

Brand
ASrock
Memory Slots
4

Rear I/O
2x Thunderbolt 4, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, 6x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-A 2.0, 1x HDMI
$579 at Amazon $490 at Newegg

If you're looking for the best Z790 motherboard for a high-end ASRock PC build, then we think the ASRock Z790 Taichi is as good as it gets. It's packed with excellent features and delivers reliable performance, building a solid platform for a high-end build. This particular motherboard features a 24+1+4 phase SPS Dr.MOS power design. It's capable of handling even the most demanding CPUs in the Raptor Lake series like the Intel Core i9-13900K. The Z790 Taichi also supports overclocking and the VRM heatsink ensures everything stays cool at all times. In addition to the RGB lights, the VRM heatsink cover and the chipset heatsink cover, both feature a unique bronze-colored gear element, exclusive to the Taichi motherboard series. Supporting the best Intel processors, the Taichi comes with four DIMM slots that let you install up to 128GB of DDR5 memory modules with speeds of up to 7200MT/s supported.

You get three full-sized reinforced x16 slots on the board in addition to an x1 slot. There are only three M.2 slots for faster SSDs, but you can install additional SATA drives with the available 6Gb/s connections. The M.2 slots are hidden under the heatsink, which should help keep the installed drives adequately cooled. The ASRock Z790 Taichi comes with a pre-installed IO shield at the back and it covers a ton of different ports. You get as many as eight USB ports at the back including two Thunderbolt 4 USB Type-C ports. There's also an HDMI port for those who want to boot without a discrete GPU. You also get two ethernet ports along with a full stack of audio ports. As a premium board, you also get plenty of headers for additional USB ports, RGB lights, fans, pumps, and more.

Source: ASRock
ASRock X670E Taichi
Best AMD

Going with AMD processors? This is the ASRock board for you.

The ASRock X670E Taichi is a high-end EATX motherboard with excellent build quality and a massive 24-stage VRM. It has support for high-speed DDR5 memory, PCIe 5.0 graphics, and multiple NVME SSDs.

Brand
ASRock
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-6600
Form Factor
E-ATX

Socket
AM5
Power Phases

Realtek ALC4082
Networking
2.5 GbE, Wi-Fi 6
Internal I/O
2x USB-A 2.0, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen1, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2x2
Rear I/O
2x USB-C 4.0, 5x USB-A 3.2 Gen2, 3x USB-A 3.2 Gen1
$400 at Newegg $415 at Micro Center

Moving onto AMD, we're recommending almost the same motherboard. The ASRock X670E Taichi could largely be considered the AMD equivalent to the Intel chipset-touting board we recommended above for Intel processors. It shares many of the same features and ports, and has the same gorgeous industrial design. It's among the best ASRock motherboards you can buy right now for AMD Ryzen processors that aren't part of the Threadripper family. Just like the Intel ASRock Taichi, the company packed this motherboard full of features. Let's start with the power delivery design, which includes a 24+2+1 phase design setup. This is more than enough to handle even the most power-hungry AMD CPUs such as the mighty AMD Ryzen 9 7950X. Then there are the usual four DIMM slots for system memory. These are capable of handling up to 128GB of DDR5-6600+.

Two PCIe 5.0 x16 slots are at hand for the most powerful graphics cards and an additional x16 slot is good to go for any expansion cards you may require. Audio is handled by the Realtek ALC4082 with an additional ESS SABRE9218 DAC for good measure. If all that wasn't enough, there are a total of four M.2 SSD slots, one being PCIe 5.0 and the rest PCIe 4.0. Eight SATA 3.0 ports are available for hooking up all 2.5-inch and 3.5-inch storage drives. Networking is lightning-fast too with Killer branding throughout the board. We've got 2.5Gb Ethernet and 802.11ax Wi-Fi 6E with Bluetooth 5.3. Connectivity is sorted too, thanks to the two USB-C 4.0, five USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, and three USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports. Just like the Intel Z790 Taichi motherboard, this is about as good as you can get with AMD and ASRock. It has the power, connectivity, and BIOS support for you to create a powerful PC.

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 LiveMixer
Best for content creators

This board will help you become the next Twitch hit

The ASRock Z790 LiveMixer, as the name suggests, is designed with creators in mind. The design is whacky, trendy, and fit for the 2020 decade. If you're building a PC to create content on, this may be just what you're looking for in terms of motherboards.

Brand
ASRock
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-7200
Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
Intel Z790
Socket
LGA 1700
Power Phases
14+1+1
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0, 2x PCIe 4.0
Storage
1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 4x PCIe 4.0 M.2, 4x SATA
Audio
Realtek ALC897
Networking
2.5 GbE
Internal I/O
1x Thunderbolt, 2x USB 3.2 Gen1, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2x2,2x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
2x USB 3.2 Gen2 Type-A Ports (10 Gb/s) - 2 x USB 3.2 Gen2 Type-C Ports (10 Gb/s) - 4 x USB 3.2 Gen1 Ports (USB32_23 are Lightning Gaming Ports. USB32_45 support Ultra USB Power.) - 6 x USB 2.0 Ports
$200 at Newegg $250 at Amazon

Don't write off this motherboard for being a mistake. The design may be polarizing, but that's how manufacturers are attempting to differentiate their products. Gaming-focused motherboards have more RGB lighting than a local airstrip and it seems graffiti artists are now being thrown into the mix with digital content creators. The end result is the whacky ASrock Z790 LiveMixer. This board is designed for content creators, video & photo editing, and game development. Because this motherboard comes with the Intel Z790 chipset and LGA 1700 socket for 12th and 13th Gen Intel processors, ASRock went all out with the RAM and PCI slots. You'll need DDR5 memory for this motherboard, supporting up to DDR5-6800. To keep everything operating stably, ASRock deployed a 14+1+1 phase power delivery system and uses high-quality Japanese capacitors.

The impressive Realtek ALC897 codec and Nahimic Audio are present too so you know just how much kit the company has thrown on. The ASRock Z790 LiveMixer makes good use of the Dragon RTL8125BG networking stack to unlock a single 2.5Gb Ethernet port. There's no Wi-Fi out of the box, but an M.2 (Key E) Socket is available for installing such a card to add wireless capabilities. With content creation, it's not uncommon to work with huge files over the network, which is where the wired connection comes into play with less latency and better performance. As well as the Key E Socket, ASRock added four PCI slots for connecting all your GPUs, capture cards, and/or expansion cards.

There's one PCIe 5.0 x16 slot, followed by two PCIe 4.0 x16 slots, and an additional PCIe 3.0 x1 slot. A total of five M.2 SSD slots are at hand with one supporting PCIe 5.0 storage. There's potential for plenty of storage capacity thanks to the four SATA 3 ports available for older 2.5- or 3.5-inch drives. The rear I/O stack is also unique for the ASRock Z790 LiveMixer motherboard, consisting of an HDMI port, DisplayPort (1.4), optical SPDIF out, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, two USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 ports, four USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, and six USB 2.0 ports. If you've got a lot of equipment to hook up to the PC, this motherboard can handle it all and then some.

Source: ASRock
ASRock WRX80 Creator
Best for enthusiasts

Upgrade your PC (and life) and AMD Ryzen Threadripper

The ASRock WRX80 is a monster of a motherboard designed specifically for AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3000 and 5000 series processors. Looking to build a powerful workstation or server? Start your build right here.

Memory Slots

$900 at Newegg $900 at Amazon

The ASRock WRX80 is an absolute monster of a motherboard. It's designed for heavy, intense applications and enthusiasts with support for the latest AMD Ryzen Threadripper processors. This thing is unlike any other motherboard we're recommending in this guide as it is capable of serving up to 64 lanes of PCIe 4.0 and has a dual-fan heatsink covering eight 105A VRM phases. Then there's another 50mm fan for the chipset. Rocking an AMD sWRX socket, you'll be able to install AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3000 and 5000 series processors. Two M.2 PCIe 4.0 slots are available for rapid storage and a further seven PCIe 4.0 x16 slots can be used for just about anything your imagination is capable of conjuring up.

It's a serious motherboard with not a drop of RGB lighting in sight. You may have noticed the eight DIMM array of RAM slots, which is capable of handling up to 2048GB (2TB!) of DDR4-4533+, and yes that capacity is not a typo. ASRock is so serious about the WRX80 Creator R2.0, the company used a 14-layer server-grade PCB, which is unheard of in the consumer motherboard segment, but this motherboard comes at a cost. The rear I/O is also incredible. You'll have the choice of using two USB-C 4.0 ports, two Mini-DisplayPort ports, four USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, and two 10Gb LAN ports. There's going to be very little you can throw at this motherboard and cause problems. If you're looking to build a powerful HEDT PC, look no further than the ASRock WRX80 CREATOR R2.0.

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi
Best value Intel

Save money with ASRock's affordable Intel platform

The ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi is designed for those who want a decent motherboard without spending too much money. It has all the right features you'll need for a capable 12th or 13th Gen Intel PC build.

We've covered some of the more powerful and even enthusiast-grade motherboard offerings from ASRock, but the company has some more affordable options available. Take the ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi, for instance. It undercuts many of the other motherboards in this ASRock collection by a substantial amount but supports 12th and 13th Gen Intel processors. The 16+1+1 phase power delivery design may not be the most powerful around, but it's still more than enough for even the Intel Core i9-13900K.

Even though this is slightly cheaper than other Z790 boards, there's still support for DDR5 RAM with speeds up to 7200MT/s. There's 2.5Gb networking, which is a nice touch and usually reserved for more expensive motherboards, even in 2023. For PCI slots, ASRock went with a three x16-slot configuration with one supporting PCIe 5.0 speeds, the second PCIe 4.0, and the third PCIe 3.0. As a bonus, there's a single M.2 (Key E) Socket for WiFi modules (it's already populated with this model).

For storage, we're looking at eight SATA 3 ports for 2.5- and 3.5-inch drives, as well as five M.2 slots for modern super-fast PCIe SSDs. One of these M.2 slots supports PCIe 5.0 speeds while the remaining four are PCIe 4.0 "only." The rear panel is fully stacked too, rocking an HDMI port, DisplayPort, optical SPDIF, one USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 port, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, and eight USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports. You may be saving some money with the ASRock Z790 Steel Legend WiFi, but it has all the important features your PC will need.

Source: ASRock
ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi
Best budget AMD

This AMD motherboard won't set fire to your wallet

You'll notice the ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi price before anything else and that shouldn't lead you to a false assumption that this motherboard won't have many of the more advanced features. It's far greater than its price tag would lead you to believe.

As well as buying more affordable motherboards for Intel processors, there are numerous budget-friendlier options for AMD too. We're picking the mighty ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi for our value recommendation. It has the slightly less headline-catching B650 chipset, compared to the flagship X670, but it's still worth checking out for a powerful AMD Ryzen 7000 series PC build. The design is almost identical to the Intel version we also recommended but with a few notable element alterations. It's a good-looking board.

To handle the more capable AMD Ryzen processors (as well as supporting some degree of overclocking), ASRock implemented a 16+2+1 phase power delivery system. The four DIMM slots are capable of handling up to DDR5-6600 RAM, which already allows for one powerful system. There may only be two x16 PCI slots, but one is running at PCIe 5.0 speeds for the latest graphics cards. Three M.2 slots are ready to go for super-fast SSD storage and one of these is also capable of handling PCIe 5.0.

Rear I/O is also a strong point, considering the price. You'll be able to play with a single HDMI port, one DisplayPort, one Optical SPDIF, one USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 port, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, four USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, and two USB-A 2.0 ports. And speaking of connectivity, there's Wi-Fi 6E and 2.5Gb Ethernet to get you online. The ASRock B650E Steel Legend WiFi shows you don't have to spend a small fortune on a motherboard to enjoy a solid experience with the latest AMD processors.

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 PG-ITX/TB4
Best compact Intel

Build your dream compact gaming PC with this Mini-ITX offering

$335 $420 Save $85

ASRock's Z790 PG-ITX/TB4 is a motherboard that punches way above its weight. You'll be limited by RAM capacity since there are only two DIMM slots, but it has an impressive list of features for compact PC builds.

Brand
ASRock
Memory Type
DDR5-7000

1x USB-A 3.2 Gen1, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2x2, 1x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
2x USB-C 4 Thunderbolt 4, 6x USB-A 3.2 Gen2
$335 at Amazon $285 at Newegg

When building a compact PC using a small form factor (SFF) case, your choice of motherboards will be limited. We're choosing the ASRock Z790 PG-ITX/TB4 as our recommendation for the Mini-ITX Intel motherboard form factor. It's a very powerful yet compact motherboard with a 10+1+1 phase power delivery design, which should be more than enough for even the Intel Core i9-13900K. The flagship Intel Z790 chipset and LGA 1700 socket ensure you're using the very best on offer.

Because of the limited size, Mini-ITX motherboards such as this only come with two DIMM slots for RAM. This means you'll only be able to install up to 64GB of capacity, but at least there's support for up to DDR5-7000. There's also one PCI slot that's capable of running at PCIe 5.0 speeds. Fear not if you have more than one M.2 SSD as this tiny motherboard actually has three M.2 slots, all three of them running at PCIe 4.0 speeds.

For networking, you'll be able to rely on either WiFi 6E or 2.5Gb Ethernet. Rear I/O isn't too shabby either. One HDMI port is joined by DisplayPort, Optical SPDIF, two USB-C 4.0 ports, and six USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports. You can build a very capable system using this motherboard with the same excellent BIOS you'd expect from ASRock.

Source: ASRock
ASRock B650E PG-ITX
Best Compact AMD

This AMD motherboard won't set fire to your wallet

The ASRock B650E PG-ITX  is an impressive compact motherboard with two M.2 slots, PCIe 5.0 support across the board, two DDR5 DIMM slots, and plenty of USB connectivity.

Brand
ASRock
Memory Slots
2
Memory Type
DDR5-6400
Form Factor
Mini-ITX
Chipset
AMD B650
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
10+2+1
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0 x16
Storage
1x PCIe 5.0 x16, 1x PCIe 4.0 x16, 2x SATA
Audio
Realtek ALC1220
Networking
2.5 GbE, Wi-Fi 6E
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2x2, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen1, 1x USB-A 2.0
$322 at Amazon $290 at Newegg

The ASRock B650E PG-ITX is our pick for the best compact ASRock AMD motherboard. It has the B650 chipset and AM5 socket, meaning you'll need to use the latest and greatest AMD processors with this motherboard, as well as DDR5 RAM. Some may view the lack of DDR4 RAM support as a drawback for AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors, but it's faster and vastly more affordable than when the generation launched. Throw in a single PCIe 5.0 x16 slot for a graphics card and two M.2 drive bays and you've got one capable compact motherboard. Being a Mini-ITX board, you will be limited by the two DIMM slots, supporting a maximum of 64GB of DDR5-6400 RAM.

Other highlights of the ASRock B650E PG-ITX include a 10+2+1 phase power delivery design, which is pretty solid for such a small motherboard. It won't allow for some record-breaking overclocking results with an AMD Ryzen 9 7950X, but it will be enough to provide reliable power at near-stock settings. This allows for a powerful, yet compact AMD system to be built. Install an AMD or Nvidia GPU from recent generations and you'll easily handle many of the latest and most-demanding PC games. 2.5GbE and Wi-Fi 6E will have you online in moments after installing your favorite OS and there are ample USB ports on the rear of the ASRock B650E PG-ITX for hooking up all your peripherals.

Choosing the best ASRock motherboard

ASRock has a lot of other motherboards in addition to the ones we've listed above. However, these are the ones we think are worth considering. We've added many Z790 motherboards like the ASRock Z790 Taichi for those looking to build a new PC based on Intel's Alder Lake and Raptor Lake CPUs. The ASRock X670E Taichi is also a great motherboard for builds with AMD processors. If you want the absolute best performance available, you're going to need an AMD Ryzen Threadripper with the mighty ASRock WRX80 Creator R2.0.

You can also check out our collection of the best LGA 1700 motherboards if you want to build a new PC with one of the new 12th or 13th-generation Intel Core processors. In that case, don't forget to take a look at our LGA 1700 CPU coolers list to find a good cooler for the new chips. Be sure to let us know what you think about our recommendations by dropping a line below. You can also join our XDA Computing Forums to get more product recommendations from our expert community.

Source: ASRock
ASRock Z790 Taichi
Best Intel

When you work and play on the same workstation, the ASRock Z790 Taichi is a solid foundation for both. It has almost everything you could ask for in an Intel PC motherboard.

$579 at Amazon $490 at Newegg
Source: ASRock
ASRock X670E Taichi
Best AMD

Going with AMD processors? This is the ASRock board for you.

The ASRock X670E Taichi is a high-end EATX motherboard with excellent build quality and a massive 24-stage VRM. It has support for high-speed DDR5 memory, PCIe 5.0 graphics, and multiple NVME SSDs.

item.title: The 5 best Steam Deck mods: Hardware and software tweaks you need to try
item.itunes:title: The 5 best Steam Deck mods: Hardware and software tweaks you need to try
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • Upgrade your storage with a microSD or SSD to have more space for games on your Steam Deck, offering a massive improvement over the default storage options.
  • Install Decky Loader to access various plugins that can enhance your SteamOS experience, providing additional functionalities and customization options.
  • Use a dock to turn your Steam Deck into a PC, allowing you to connect peripherals, such as a mouse and keyboard, and even use it for couch gaming or as a portable gaming console.

The Steam Deck being analogous to a laptop but in handheld form makes it a viable device for modding, and that's exactly what many people have done. While it's a gaming device first and foremost, you can do basically anything on it thanks to the control that Valve hands over to the users. Not only can you modify the software running on it, but you can make hardware modifications too and even install another operating system if you wish. If you're looking for ideas, we have a few for you below.

1 Replace your SSD to get more storage for more games

One of the most important upgrades

If you want more storage on your Steam Deck, you can buy a microSD or SSD and install it yourself. The Steam Deck takes an M.2 2230 SSD, which you can typically get in up to 2TB of storage. That's a massive upgrade over the official 512GB SSD that comes in the highest-tier option, and you can save a bit of money by going for one of the lower-tier devices and then buying your own SSD for installation instead.

More storage means more games, and given that the purpose of the Steam Deck is gaming first and foremost, then this is one of the best upgrades you can make for your shiny handheld. It's a little bit more involved than some of the other mods you may want to do, but it's certainly one of the most immediately rewarding.

2 Install Decky Loader to get plugins that enhance the SteamOS experience

Hack your Steam Deck the way Valve intended

If you've never heard of Decky Loader before, it's a launcher for the Steam Deck that gives you easy access to browse and install a variety of plugins. By installing it, users will gain access to plugins that can be found through the Quick Access menu. There are a ton of options available, including FreeGames for free game notifications, Power Tools, ProtonDB badges, and more.

This is arguably one of the safest mods you can do, as Valve knows that Linux users will want to tinker with devices. There's a lot you can do with Decky Loader, and they can really enhance the Steam Deck experience.

3 Get a dock and use your Steam Deck as a PC

It also works for couch gaming, too

The Steam Deck being a bona fide PC (it is just Linux, after all) means that you can use it as one with the appropriate peripherals, including a great dock. You can use it to add USB-A ports for accessories like a mouse and keyboard, Ethernet support, and HDMI or DisplayPort for connecting monitors or other displays. It greatly increases the capabilities of the Steam Deck.

If you don't want to use it as a PC, you can basically turn it into a gaming console via a dock, akin to a Nintendo Switch. Any of the best Steam Deck controllers will also work with it, but other controllers will work as long as they support Bluetooth. I use a Nintendo Switch Pro Controller over Bluetooth with mine when sitting on the couch. This way, I can play games like Wolfenstein: The New Order or Cyberpunk: 2077 on the couch, something that I typically couldn't do before as I don't own any consoles.

4 Emulate all your favorite consoles

Owning a Steam Deck can also mean you don't need to carry your other handhelds or hook up your retro consoles. I have a PS2, for example, along with other old-school consoles, and thanks to the openness of the Steam Deck, I can take all of those devices with me. I can play a Pokèmon game on my Steam Deck instead of taking two consoles with me when I'm out and about.

Of course, emulation software like EmuDeck takes a little time to set up, but it's a relatively straightforward process. Following on from that, dumping your own games to play on it will be fairly easy, with the biggest hiccup many run into being the process of transferring files to the Steam Deck. Once you're set up, though, you'll be able to take an even larger library of games with you everywhere.

5 Install Windows

Like the other gaming handhelds, but worse

The Steam Deck, being a handheld PC, can install and boot Windows, essentially turning it into something like the Asus ROG Ally. We don't recommend it because it takes a lot to set up, but it will allow you to play games that require a Windows-based anti-cheat on your device. What's more, you'll be able to execute Windows-based applications if that's something that appeals to you.

There aren't many reasons you would want to install Windows, particularly because Proton is as good as it is. In fact, it actually makes some games run better on Linux than on Windows. Still, you can do it if you want, and Valve has drivers that will allow it to work properly too. If you want a Windows gaming handheld though, then there are better options than the Steam Deck.

Final thoughts

There are a lot of other mods or customizations you can perform on your Steam Deck. Those are the ones we recommend starting with, but you can find other ideas online if you know where to look. The Steam Deck subreddit is a great place to start. You can also put your Steam Deck in desktop mode and browse through the programs you can install under the Discover tab. The console itself can offer many options itself, so explore and see what you can find.

Steam Deck

Valve's first portable gaming PC, the Steam Deck, is one of the coolest handheld gaming consoles on the market, with a huge library of games to play and the ability to take them on the go.

$400 at Steam

What's more, you'll be able to execute Windows-based applications if that's something that appeals to you.

There aren't many reasons you would want to install Windows, particularly because Proton is as good as it is. In fact, it actually makes some games run better on Linux than on Windows. Still, you can do it if you want, and Valve has drivers that will allow it to work properly too. If you want a Windows gaming handheld though, then there are better options than the Steam Deck.

Final thoughts

There are a lot of other mods or customizations you can perform on your Steam Deck. Those are the ones we recommend starting with, but you can find other ideas online if you know where to look. The Steam Deck subreddit is a great place to start. You can also put your Steam Deck in desktop mode and browse through the programs you can install under the Discover tab. The console itself can offer many options itself, so explore and see what you can find.

Steam Deck

Valve's first portable gaming PC, the Steam Deck, is one of the coolest handheld gaming consoles on the market, with a huge library of games to play and the ability to take them on the go.

$400 at Steam

item.title: Maingear is launching high-performance workstations for creative professionals
item.itunes:title: Maingear is launching high-performance workstations for creative professionals
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • Maingear has released a new line of high-performance workstations designed to meet the intensive tasks of creative professionals, including video game developers and graphic designers.
  • The Pro Series Workstation PC line consists of three models, with options for Intel Core, Intel Xeon, AMD Ryzen, and AMD Ryzen Threadripper CPUs. The workstations support multiple Nvidia GPUs and offer up to 256GB DDR5 memory.
  • The line includes a mid-tower model and two full tower models, with various configuration options for storage, cooling, and operating system. Maingear's website provides a configuration tool for pricing and customization.

Creative professionals ranging from video game developers to graphic designers often have to tackle a wide range of intensive tasks on their PCs. That being said, their hardware often takes an ample amount of wear and tear, even over a short period of time. To cater to these types of workloads, Maingear is now rolling out a new line of high-performance workstations, which could benefit those in creative industries.

The Pro Series Workstation PC line from Maingear was announced on November 21, and it consists of three new workstations that can be configured to meet your specific needs. The ProWS features a traditional mid-tower design, and you can choose from an Intel Core or AMD Ryzen CPU. It has Noctua case fans for cooling, and you can opt for up to two Nvidia RTX 6000 GPUs for your graphics needs. You’ll also get 128GB DDR5 memory, and you’ll have a choice of Samsung 990 NVMe SSDs or a Seagate IronWolf HDD for storage. In terms of the OS, this workstation comes with the option of Windows 11 Pro, Linux, or both in a dual-boot configuration, and it has a 1600W power supply unit.

If you opt for the ProWS Max or Pro RS, you will get a bit of an upgrade — for starters, these models have a full tower. You can also choose from the Intel Core, Intel Xeon, AMD Ryzen, AMD Ryzen Threadripper, and AMD Ryzen Threadripper Pro CPUs. In terms of graphics, the workstations support up to four Nvidia A4000 or A5000 GPUs or three RTX 6000 GPUs. You’ll notice a memory upgrade as well — up to 256GB DDR5 — compared to the 128GB DDR5 that comes with the ProWS.

Source: Maingear

For more information on pricing and all of the line’s features, Maingear’s website has a configuration tool that can help you determine which workstation is best for your needs. Investing in one of these new options might be just the kickstarter for your next big creative project.

You can also choose from the Intel Core, Intel Xeon, AMD Ryzen, AMD Ryzen Threadripper, and AMD Ryzen Threadripper Pro CPUs. In terms of graphics, the workstations support up to four Nvidia A4000 or A5000 GPUs or three RTX 6000 GPUs. You’ll notice a memory upgrade as well — up to 256GB DDR5 — compared to the 128GB DDR5 that comes with the ProWS.

Source: Maingear

For more information on pricing and all of the line’s features, Maingear’s website has a configuration tool that can help you determine which workstation is best for your needs. Investing in one of these new options might be just the kickstarter for your next big creative project.

item.title: Best deals on Samsung Galaxy S23 series in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best deals on Samsung Galaxy S23 series in 2023

item.itunes:summary:
The Samsung Galaxy S23 series are easily among the best phones you can buy right now. And while the phones are pretty expensive, you don't have to pay full price if you keep an eye out for great deals. With that said, we've rounded up the best Galaxy S23 series deals from retailers and wireless carriers, so you can easily save lots of money without having to wade through the internet.

The Samsung Galaxy S23 series starts at $799 in the U.S. for the base Galaxy S23. The Galaxy S23+ begins at $999, while the top-of-the-line Galaxy S23 Ultra is available at a starting price of $1,199. The Galaxy S23 series handsets aren't radically different from their respective predecessors, but they bring some noteworthy changes to the table like an improved processor, upgraded cameras, and slight alterations to already sleek designs.

Samsung Galaxy S23 series: Best deals on unlocked models

You can purchase the unlocked variants of the Galaxy S23, Galaxy S23+, and Galaxy S23 Ultra through Samsung's website, Amazon, and Best Buy. Use the links below to snag attractive bonuses on your purchase.

Samsung Store

Get a free storage upgrade when you trade in an eligible device on purchase of the flagship Galaxy S23 Ultra through Samsung's official web store. If you go with the cheaper models of the Galaxy S23, you'll get up to $615 in instant trade-in credit for your old device.

Samsung Galaxy S23

Save $100 on the Samsung Galaxy S23 and grab it for just $700 from Amazon by following the link below.

If you want to subsidize your new phone's cost by opting for a carrier-locked contract, you should check out the listings below for the best Galaxy S23 deals on carrier-locked models. The lineup is available through all major carriers in the U.S., including AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon.

AT&T

AT&T subscribers can get up to $800 off via bill credits over 36 months with an eligible trade-in and unlimited plan on the purchase of a new Galaxy S23, Galaxy S23+, or Galaxy S23 Ultra.

$1200 at AT&T

T-Mobile

Buy the Galaxy S23, Galaxy S23+, or Galaxy S23 Ultra through T-Mobile and get up to $800 off on the device when you activate a new line on T-Mobile's Go5G Plus plan.

Samsung Galaxy S23

Grab the Galaxy S23 through T-Mobile and get up to $800 off when you activate a new line on the Go5G Plus plan.
